actual.notes: Very windy, more gusty than Mar 7, very easterly direction made launch and landing very difficult

verdict.equipment_notes: 2.2m was too small at 19-20kts with gusty E conditions. Rode 2.8m instead — better power management through lulls while still depowerable in gusts.

calibration_notes.verdict_correction: Verdict was correct (GO) but underestimated wind strength again
calibration_notes.pattern: Second consecutive day where actual wind significantly exceeded iKTRRM forecast. Strong pressure gradient (4.0 hPa to N buoy) + clear morning skies + strong thermal (Δ4.7°C) combined to overshoot. Upside miss identified in pre-session analysis was correct.
calibration_notes.gustiness: Higher gust ratio than forecast — 1.35x at 11am reading, rider reported more gusty during session
calibration_notes.direction_issue: Easterly rotation made launch/landing very difficult — same wind shadow pattern as Mar 4-6 but with stronger wind making it harder
calibration_notes.key_learning: Two consecutive days of overshooting iKTRRM by 15-20%. When pressure gradient is 'strong' (>3 hPa), iKTRRM appears to systematically underforecast Kanaha by ~3kts. Consider adding a gradient-based bias correction to iKTRRM forecasts.

observations.free_text: Similar session to March 7 — very windy, more gusty. Very easterly direction made launch and landing very difficult. Wind shadow pattern persists in E conditions with strong trades.
observations.comparison: Near-identical to Mar 7: strong trades, E rotation, wind shadow. Gustier today.
